assertions.query: ALTER USER user1 WITH PASSWORD NULL;
CREATE USER IF NOT EXISTS user1 PASSWORD 'hello3';
CREATE ROLE IF NOT EXISTS user2 PASSWORD 'hi1' LOGIN;
CREATE ROLE IF NOT EXISTS user2 PASSWORD 'hi3' LOGIN;
CREATE TABLE test (pk INT4 PRIMARY KEY);
SELECT * FROM test ORDER BY pk
SELECT * FROM test ORDER BY pk
GRANT SELECT ON test TO user2;
SELECT * FROM test ORDER BY pk
REVOKE SELECT ON test FROM user2;
SELECT * FROM test ORDER BY pk
GRANT SELECT ON test TO PUBLIC;
SELECT * FROM test ORDER BY pk
SELECT * FROM test ORDER BY pk;
WITH cte AS (SELECT * FROM test ORDER BY pk) SELECT * FROM cte;
GRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE ON test TO user1;
SELECT * FROM test ORDER BY pk;
WITH cte AS (SELECT * FROM test ORDER BY pk) SELECT * FROM cte;
SELECT * FROM test ORDER BY pk;
REVOKE SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE ON test FROM user1;
SELECT * FROM test ORDER BY pk;

assertions.scriptName: CREATE privilege ON SEQUENCE and ROUTINE
assertions.query: CREATE FUNCTION testfunc3() RETURNS int AS $$ BEGIN RETURN 3; END; $$ LANGUAGE plpgsql

assertions.scriptName: CREATE privilege ON SEQUENCE and ROUTINE
assertions.query: CREATE PROCEDURE interpreted_example_3(input TEXT) AS $$ BEGIN INSERT INTO test VALUES ('3' || input); END; $$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
